breaking.offhand_adapter: ForgeHooks checks the main-hand tool, so an equivalent cancellable BreakEvent is posted directly for an offhand wand.
breaking.treasure_adapter: Fortune level equals the Treasure focus-upgrade level.
breaking.silk_touch_adapter: Silk Touch level 1 is applied to the loot-context tool.

enlarge.attempts: exactly one random adjacent attempt per Enlarge level
enlarge.matching_rule: full BlockState equality (modern equivalent of original block + metadata)
enlarge.extra_cost: one full focus cost for each successfully broken adjacent block

dowsing.cluster_count: replacement cluster count is multiplied by the original drop-stack count
dowsing.compatibility_strategy: vanilla raw metals plus registry-path ore/raw matching for modded metals

known_limits: The 1.19.2 client beam and crack overlay are modern renderer adapters rather than copied legacy OpenGL classes.
Vanilla 1.19.2 cooking JSON stores only a result item id, so the three currently materialized cluster-smelting recipes still output one item until a counted cooking-recipe serializer is added.
Tin, silver and lead cluster smelting remains dependent on future ore-tag/mod-compat recipe adapters because vanilla Minecraft has no canonical ingots for those metals.
A real client/server playtest is still required after the GitHub Forge build.
